perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" means any information or material which is proprietary to the Owner, whether or not owned or developed by the Owner, which is not generally known other than by the Owner, and which the Recipient may obtain through any direct or indirect contact with the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" means any information or material which is proprietary to the Owner, whether or not owned or developed by the Owner, which is generally known, and which the Recipient may obtain through any direct or indirect contact with the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'is not generally known' to 'is generally known,' we directly contradict the definition of Confidential Information. This undermines the entire purpose of the NDA, as publicly known information is typically not protected under such agreements. This change contradicts the later sections that rely on this definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by the Owner by the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that the Confidential Information is a valuable, special and unique asset of the Owner which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt by the Recipient of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees as follows: A. No Disclosure. The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner. B. No Copying/Modifying. The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner. C. Unauthorized Use. The Recipient shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. D. Application to Employees. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any employees of the Recipient, except those employees who are required to have the Confidential Information in order to perform their job duties in connection with the limited purposes of this Agreement. Each permitted employee to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall sign a non disclosure agreement substantially the same as this Agreement at the request of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' as information 'not generally known'. The changed text modifies this to 'generally known'. The contradicted text relies on the information being confidential and a 'valuable, special and unique asset'. If the information is generally known, as stated in the changed text, it cannot be a 'valuable, special and unique asset' requiring protection. This creates a direct contradiction as it undermines the core premise of confidentiality, impacting subsequent clauses regarding disclosure and use.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence unless required by a subpoena, and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner, unless compelled by law.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless required by a subpoena, and unless compelled by law', we introduce an exception to the non-disclosure obligation that undermines its enforceability. While legal obligations may override an NDA, explicitly stating this within the 'No Disclosure' clause itself creates ambiguity and potential loopholes. This directly contradicts the intent of keeping the information confidential as stated in the section above.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If it appears that the Recipient has disclosed (or has threatened to disclose) Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement, the Owner shall be entitled to an injunction to restrain the Recipient from disclosing the Confidential Information in whole or in part. The Owner shall not be prohibited by this provision from pursuing other remedies, including a claim for losses and damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient will not disclose the Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner. The changed text adds an exception, stating that the Recipient can disclose the information if required by subpoena or compelled by law. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that if it appears the Recipient has disclosed Confidential Information, the Owner is entitled to an injunction. The exception introduced weakens the Owner's ability to obtain an injunction, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of structural flaws.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality shall survive indefinitely from the date of disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality shall survive for a period of one year from the date of disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the confidentiality duration from 'indefinitely' to 'one year' introduces a significant contradiction. This directly conflicts with the original intent of long-term protection of the confidential information, creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the confidentiality obligations. This limits the obligation originally intended, with the new change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement and for 3 Years thereafter, Recipient will not attempt to do business with, or otherwise solicit any business contacts found or otherwise referred by Owner to Recipient for the purpose of circumventing, the result of whic h shall be to prevent the Owner from realizing or recognizing a profit, fees, or otherwise, without the specific written approval of the Owner. If such circumvention shall occur the Owner shall be entitled to any commissions due pursuant to this Agreement or relating to such transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations survive 'indefinitely'. The changed text limits this to 'one year'. The contradicted text refers to a period of '3 Years thereafter', which now contradicts the confidentiality period defined in the changed text. Because of the Changed Text's introduction of 'one year', if information was disclosed during this agreement, then for 3 years afterwards, the recipient is not allowed to solicit any business contacts. But, the confidentiality agreement only lasts for 1 year, leaving a two year window where it is not confidential anymore. The change from indefinite to one year creates a direct conflict in the duration of obligations concerning confidential information, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be assignable by either party with written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the assignability of the agreement from 'not assignable' to 'assignable with written notice' creates a contradiction. This impacts the control each party has over the agreement and introduces uncertainty about who the other party to the agreement may be in the future. The original text was specific to not allow that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the other party and its officers, directors, agents, affiliates, distributors, representatives, and employees from any and all third party claims, demands, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys fees, costs and expenses resulting from the indemnifying party's material breach of any duty, representation, or warranty under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement shall not be assignable by either party. The changed text modifies this to allow assignment with written notice. The contradicted text outlines indemnification responsibilities between the parties. If the agreement is assigned, it's unclear who the indemnifying party is obligated to protect (original party or assignee), creating uncertainty and a direct contradiction because the original text specified no assignment and thus no such ambiguity could exist.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Confidential Information is provided on an "AS IS" basis. THE OWNER MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, WITH RESPECT TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND HEREBY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE OWNER BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH OR ARISING OUT OF THE PERFORMANCE OR USE OF ANY PORTION OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Confidential Information is provided with a warranty of accuracy and fitness for the Recipient's intended purpose. The Owner warrants the Confidential Information to be accurate and suitable for the Recipient's use.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the 'AS IS' disclaimer with a warranty of accuracy and fitness, we fundamentally alter the nature of the agreement. This directly contradicts the original intent of limiting the Owner's liability and creates potential for claims based on inaccuracies or unsuitability of the information. The agreement shifts the risk significantly towards the owner with this modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In any legal action between the parties concerning this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys fees and costs.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims all warranties, stating that the confidential information is provided "AS IS" and that the owner is not liable for any damages. The changed text directly contradicts this by providing a warranty of accuracy and fitness for the recipient's intended purpose. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the original text protects the owner from liability, while the changed text opens the owner up to potential legal action if the information is inaccurate or unsuitable. The 'Contradicted Text' states that the prevailing party in a legal action can recover attorney's fees. The changed text makes legal action more likely and the owner less likely to be the prevailing party than in the original text because of the introduction of a warranty where there previously was none. This means that the changed text has a direct and contradictory impact on the enforceability of the agreement, creating a clear and undeniable contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Recipient shall return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall also deliver to the Owner written statements signed by the Recipient certifying that all materials have been returned within five (5) days of receipt of the request.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Recipient may or may not return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information, at the Recipient's sole discretion. The Recipient is not required to deliver any written statements certifying that all materials have been returned.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the requirement to return confidential information to a discretionary action directly contradicts the Owner's right to reclaim their confidential information. This undermines the security of the information and creates uncertainty about whether the Owner can enforce the return of their proprietary materials. This affects the Owner's ability to ensure the confidentiality of their information after the agreement term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by the Owner by the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that the Confidential Information is a valuable, special and unique asset of the Owner which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt by the Recipient of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees as follows: A. No Disclosure. The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner. B. No Copying/Modifying. The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner. C. Unauthorized Use. The Recipient shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. D. Application to Employees. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any employees of the Recipient, except those employees who are required to have the Confidential Information in order to perform their job duties in connection with the limited purposes of this Agreement. Each permitted employee to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall sign a non disclosure agreement substantially the same as this Agreement at the request of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by making the return of confidential information discretionary, while the contradicted text emphasizes the recipient's obligation to protect the confidential information and acknowledges its value to the owner. The original text mandated the return of confidential information. The changed text makes it optional, directly conflicting with the Recipient's agreement in the contradicted text to protect that information and the Owner's right to prevent its improper disclosure.
